rationale: In the literature review, Moe (2006) is not just listed; it is singled out as the notable exception within two-stage choice models because it uses individual-level clickstream data for browsed products. The citing paper uses this point to frame its gap and motivate a different approach based on aggregate online search-network data for durable goods. At the same time, the paper does not directly reuse Moe's two-stage model or estimation procedure, so the role is substantive positioning rather than direct methodological inheritance.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Moe (2006) as a key reference point in the consideration-set/search stream, emphasizing it as the clickstream-based exception among two-stage models. It then positions its own contribution as an alternative approach for durable-goods search mapping using aggregate product-link data.
